title: Ship Recycling Milestone Tackling Oversupply And Overage Challenges
section_path: Ship Recycling Milestone Tackling Oversupply And Overage Challenges
section_path_text: Ship Recycling Milestone Tackling Oversupply And Overage Challenges
summary:
Main
Hong Kong Convention Enters into Force After 16 Years
First adopted in Hong Kong in 2009, the Convention's implementation has been a long and complex process, reflecting years of negotiation, debate, and determination from governments, regulators, industry stakeholders, and NGOs. While India leads with over 110 yards compliant with the Convention, Bangladesh and Turkey show limited readiness, and Pakistan currently has none. Recently announced funding to upgrade Pakistan's Gadani shipbreaking facilities highlights ongoing regional challenges.
